title: Format-agnostic validation: per-issue content_format + repo default + production parser dispatch
description:
Make production validation format-agnostic: select the content parser per issue, defaulting to a per-repo setting, so HTML/XML parsers are exercised in production rather than always parsing Markdown.

## Background

Part of Scope Expansion 2. Epic-review finding: production hardcodes `MarkdownContentParser`, defeating the format-agnostic design. See `dev/active/f6a704d0-expansion2-plan.md` §2 and §6a (MF5).

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] An `Issue` carries an optional `content_format` field (`markdown|html|xml`), serde default+skip so existing issue files are not rewritten; settable via `jit issue create/update --content-format` and reflected in `--json` and `jit --schema` (MCP parity).
- [ ] [hard] A per-repo default `content_format` (default markdown) exists in config.toml and survives later backward-compat removal (behavioral key).
- [ ] [hard] A single selector chooses the parser as issue.content_format → repo default → markdown, and is used at BOTH production projection sites (`local.rs` build_projection AND `graph.rs` criterion_ids); no production site hardcodes Markdown.
- [ ] [hard] A selected format whose parser feature is not compiled in produces a clear error (no silent Markdown fallback).
- [ ] [hard] CI executes the cross-format path via a `cargo test -p jit --features html,xml` job (it is not run today); end-to-end tests prove HTML/XML bodies are parsed by the selected parser in production.
- [ ] [hard] `cargo fmt`, `clippy --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo test --workspace`, and `cargo test -p jit --features html,xml` are clean.
